width.description: VIEWPORT design width in pixels (the browser frame, NOT the world). 9:16 PORTRAIT (432x768) so Scale.FIT FILLS on a portrait phone and LETTERBOXES centered on a landscape laptop — one fixed canvas plays on both mobile and desktop. The WORLD a level occupies is BaseLevelScene.mapWidth/mapHeight, which may be WIDER than the viewport (an N-screen scrolling level) — the camera follows the player so the viewport stays full at every scroll position; for a single-screen level mapWidth == this viewport width. OWNED BY core/gameConfig.json (the infra groups) — a module MUST mirror core's screenSize, never diverge (template-contract §4).

height.description: VIEWPORT design height in pixels. 9:16 portrait (432x768) — see width. The WORLD height is BaseLevelScene.mapHeight (normally one screen for a side-scroller); it may exceed this for a vertical-scrolling level, camera follows. OWNED BY core (template-contract §4).

gravityY.description: Downward gravity applied to the player (px/s^2)

timeLimit.description: Level countdown length in SECONDS for the failModel:'time' family. BaseLevelScene counts down from this to timeRemaining<=0 (=> status:'lost'); inert unless failModel === 'time'.
